title: arabis auriculata var. sinaica
intro: Arabis auriculata var. sinaica, also known as golden rockcress, is a perennial herb native to China, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, Arabis auriculata var. sinaica has been used in medicine due to its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs and respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, Arabis auriculata var. sinaica can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
